controls.description: The security awareness program is reviewed at least once every 12 months, and updated as needed to address any new threats and vulnerabilities that may impact the security of the entity's CDE, or information provided to personnel about their role in protecting cardholder data.

controls.title: Personnel receive security awareness training upon hire and at least once every 12 months
controls.description: Personnel receive security awareness training as follows: upon hire, at least once every 12 months, and the training includes awareness of threats, vulnerabilities, and risks to the CDE, an acknowledgement by personnel that they have read and understood the information security policy and procedures.

controls.title: Security awareness training includes awareness of threats and vulnerabilities that could impact the security of the CDE including phishing and related attacks and social engineering
controls.description: Security awareness training includes awareness of threats and vulnerabilities that could impact the security of the CDE, including but not limited to phishing and related attacks, and social engineering.

controls.title: Security awareness training includes awareness of acceptable use of end-user technologies
controls.description: Security awareness training includes awareness of acceptable use of end-user technologies in accordance with Requirement 12.2.1.

controls.title: Potential personnel who will have access to the CDE are screened prior to hire to minimize the risk of attacks from internal sources
controls.description: Potential personnel who will have access to the CDE are screened prior to hire to minimize the risk of attacks from internal sources.

controls.title: A list of all third-party service providers with which account data is shared, or that could affect the security of account data, is maintained
controls.description: A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared, or that could affect the security of account data, is maintained, including a description for each of the services provided.

controls.title: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained that include acknowledgements that TPSPs are responsible for the security of account data
controls.description: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained that include acknowledgements from TPSPs that TPSPs are responsible for the security of account data the TPSPs possess or otherwise store, process, or transmit on behalf of the entity, or to the extent that they could impact the security of the entity's CDE.

controls.title: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs including proper due diligence prior to engagement
controls.description: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence prior to engagement.

controls.title: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs' PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months
controls.description: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs' PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months.

controls.title: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the entity
controls.description: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between both the TPSP and the entity.

controls.title: Additional requirement for TPSPs: TPSPs acknowledge in writing to customers that they are responsible for the security of account data they possess or otherwise store, process, or transmit on behalf of the customer
controls.description: Additional requirement for TPSPs only: TPSPs acknowledge in writing to customers that they are responsible for the security of account data the TPSP possesses or otherwise stores, processes, or transmits on behalf of the customer, or to the extent that they could impact the security of the customer's CDE.

controls.title: Additional requirement for TPSPs: TPSPs support their customers' requests for information to meet Requirements 12.8.4 and 12.8.5
controls.description: Additional requirement for TPSPs only: TPSPs support their customers' requests for information to meet Requirements 12.8.4 and 12.8.5 by providing upon customer request relevant compliance status information and details about the PCI DSS requirements that are the responsibility of the TPSP.

controls.title: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident
controls.description: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident. The plan includes roles, responsibilities, and communication and contact strategies in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident, including notification of the payment brands and acquirers at a minimum; incident response procedures, business recovery and continuity procedures, data backup processes, analysis of legal requirements for reporting compromises, coverage and responses of all critical system components, and reference or inclusion of incident response procedures from the payment brands.

controls.title: At least once every 12 months, the security incident response plan is reviewed and the content is updated as needed
controls.description: At least once every 12 months, the security incident response plan is reviewed and the content is updated as needed, and tested, including all elements listed in Requirement 12.10.1.

controls.title: Specific personnel are designated to be available on a 24/7 basis to respond to suspected or confirmed security incidents
controls.description: Specific personnel are designated to be available on a 24/7 basis to respond to suspected or confirmed security incidents.

controls.title: Personnel responsible for responding to suspected and confirmed security incidents are appropriately and periodically trained on their incident response responsibilities
controls.description: Personnel responsible for responding to suspected and confirmed security incidents are appropriately and periodically trained on their incident response responsibilities.

controls.title: The frequency of periodic training for incident response personnel is defined in the entity's targeted risk analysis
controls.description: The frequency of periodic training for incident response personnel is defined in the entity's targeted risk analysis, which is performed according to all elements specified in Requirement 12.3.1.

controls.title: The security incident response plan includes monitoring and responding to alerts from security monitoring systems
controls.description: The security incident response plan includes monitoring and responding to alerts from security monitoring systems, including but not limited to intrusion-detection and intrusion-prevention systems, network security controls, change-detection mechanisms for critical files, and the change- and tamper-detection mechanism for payment pages.

controls.title: The security incident response plan is modified and evolved according to lessons learned and to incorporate industry developments
controls.description: The security incident response plan is modified and evolved according to lessons learned and to incorporate industry developments.

controls.title: Incident response procedures are in place, to be initiated upon the detection of stored PAN anywhere it is not expected
controls.description: Incident response procedures are in place, to be initiated upon the detection of stored PAN anywhere it is not expected, and include determining what to do if PAN is discovered outside the CDE, including its retrieval, secure deletion, and/or migration into the currently defined CDE, as applicable; identifying the sensitive authentication data stored with PAN; determining where the account data came from and how it ended up where it was not expected; and remediating data leaks or process gaps that resulted in the account data being where it was not expected.
